edges.description: Several paradigmatic subtypes share white-matter injury and neurodegenerative propagation.
edges.target: White Matter Degeneration and Secondary Demyelination

edges.description: Some subtypes, especially SPAX5, are driven by mitochondrial proteostasis failure.
White-matter and cerebellar system injury contribute to the core ataxic phenotype.
edges.source: White Matter Degeneration and Secondary Demyelination

edges.description: Pyramidal tract dysfunction contributes to spastic clinical features.
edges.source: White Matter Degeneration and Secondary Demyelination

edges.description: Combined cerebellar and pyramidal-system injury produces gait impairment.
edges.source: White Matter Degeneration and Secondary Demyelination

edges.description: Purkinje neuron and cerebellar dysfunction produce ataxia.
Mitochondrial neurodegeneration in SPAX5 includes spastic features.

evidence.reference_title: Sustained OMA1-mediated integrated stress response is beneficial for spastic ataxia type 5
evidence.snippet: Heterozygous AFG3L2 mutations cause spinocerebellar ataxia type 28 (SCA28) or dominant optic atrophy type 12 (DOA12), while biallelic AFG3L2 mutations result in the rare and severe spastic ataxia type 5 (SPAX5).

nodes.description: Cerebellar ataxia is a defining feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Reviewing the genetic causes of spastic-ataxias.
evidence.snippet: Although the combined presence of ataxia and pyramidal features has a long differential, the presence of a true spastic-ataxia as the predominant clinical syndrome has a rather limited differential diagnosis.

nodes.description: Gait impairment is a major functional manifestation of the combined ataxic and spastic syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: IMPACT, a Supervised Rehabilitation Program for Spastic Ataxias: A Rater-blinded, Randomized Controlled Trial
evidence.snippet: Spastic ataxias are a group of diseases causing symptoms such as walking difficulties and balance impairments that lead to a high risk of falls.

nodes.description: The syndrome represents a clinically coherent but genetically diverse neurodegenerative phenotype in which ataxia and pyramidal-spastic features co-occur.

evidence.reference_title: Reviewing the genetic causes of spastic-ataxias.
evidence.snippet: Although the combined presence of ataxia and pyramidal features has a long differential, the presence of a true spastic-ataxia as the predominant clinical syndrome has a rather limited differential diagnosis.

nodes.description: In the SPAX5 subtype, biallelic AFG3L2 dysfunction causes mitochondrial proteotoxic stress and activates the OMA1-DELE1-HRI integrated stress response pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Sustained OMA1-mediated integrated stress response is beneficial for spastic ataxia type 5
evidence.snippet: In this work, we demonstrated that mitochondrial proteotoxicity in the absence/mutation of AFG3L2 activates the OMA1-DELE1-HRI pathway eliciting the integrated stress response.

nodes.description: Pyramidal or spastic motor features are the other defining clinical component of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Reviewing the genetic causes of spastic-ataxias.
evidence.snippet: Although the combined presence of ataxia and pyramidal features has a long differential, the presence of a true spastic-ataxia as the predominant clinical syndrome has a rather limited differential diagnosis.

nodes.description: In paradigmatic SPAX disorders, especially ARSACS, widespread white matter damage with secondary demyelination contributes to the spastic-ataxic phenotype.
nodes.id: White Matter Degeneration and Secondary Demyelination

evidence.reference_title: An MRI evaluation of white matter involvement in paradigmatic forms of spastic ataxia: results from the multi-center PROSPAX study
evidence.snippet: In ARSACS, but not SPG7 patients, we observed a complex and multi-faced involvement of brain WM, with a clinically meaningful widespread loss of axonal and dendritic integrity, secondary demyelination and, overall, a reduction in cellularity and volume.
